---
title: CME Problem Set 2
date: last-modified
author: Breno Nacif
format:
  pdf:
    code-fold: true
include-in-header:
  text: |
    \usepackage{fvextra}
    \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{Highlighting}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
execute: 
  fig-format: png
engine: julia
---

## Setup & Packages
First, we activate the project and load the necessary packages.

```{julia}
#| label: setup
#| message: false
#| output: false

# activate local Project.toml to manage package versions
import Pkg
Pkg.activate(".")

# load packages
using Interpolations, DataInterpolations, Distributions, Random,
      DataFrames, Plots, PrettyTables, Roots, BenchmarkTools
```

# Question 1
## (a)
We want to guess the life expectancy at birth for someone born in 1996.
To do so, observe that life expectancy increases monotonically with time according to the observed data.
```{julia}
#| label: q1-data

# Storing the data using a Dictionary
life_expectancy = Dict(
    "year" => [1940, 1950, 1960, 1970, 1980, 1991, 2000, 2010],
    "expectancy" => [45.5, 48.0, 52.5, 57.6, 62.5, 66.9, 71.1, 74.4]
)

# a) Best guess for life expectancy at birth for someone born in 1996
# we use PCHIP (Monotonic) because life expectancy behaves monotonically and has a slight concavity that Splines might overshoot.
pchip_interp_life_exp = interpolate(
    Int64.(life_expectancy["year"]), 
    life_expectancy["expectancy"], 
    FritschButlandMonotonicInterpolation()
)

guess_1996 = pchip_interp_life_exp(1996)
println("The best guess for life expectancy in 1996 is $(round(guess_1996, digits=2)) years.")

```

## (b)
Now we plot the series of life expectancy filling the missing years using Pchip interpolation again.

```{julia}
#| label: q1-plot

# b) Plotting the life expectancy using the interpolation method
years_plot = 1940:1:2010

plot(life_expectancy["year"], life_expectancy["expectancy"], 
     seriestype=:scatter, 
     label="Data",
     ylabel="Life Expectancy at birth", 
     legend=:topleft)
plot!(years_plot, pchip_interp_life_exp.(years_plot), label="Pchip")

```


# Question 2
## (a)
